sections.paragraph:
fut. יִיעַף-

(1) i.q. Arab. وغف to go swiftly, to run. See Hophal, and the derived nouns יְעָף and תֹּועָפֹות.

(2) to be fatigued, wearied out (compare עָיַף), whether in running, Jerem. 2:24 , מְבַקְּשֶׁיהָ לֹא־יִיעָפוּ “those who seek her will not be weary.” Luth. die fie fuchen, dürfen nicht weit laufen. Isa 40:30, 31 Isa 40:31or with heavy labour, Isa 40:28 44:12 Hab 2:13 or with grief, Isa 50:4. [But this is the next word, the derivative יָעֵף.]

Hophal, part. wearied. Dan 9:21, מֻעָף בִּיעָף “wearied in flight.” LXX. τάχει φερόμενος. Others, following Theod., Vulg., Syr., take מוּעָף from the root עוּף, and translate it flying, but this is unsuitable to that which follows בִּיעָף.

Derivatives, תֹּועָפֹות and the following words.
